stories.headline: Apple ships a new Siri that can actually hold a conversation
stories.blurb: Two years after promising a smarter assistant, Apple just launched what it calls an 'entirely new version of Siri' — one that's more conversational and more capable than the original. The update is part of a broader refresh to Apple Intelligence, the company's AI platform that debuted but never quite delivered on its early promises.
stories.why_it_matters: Apple's assistant might finally work the way people expected it to in 2023.
stories.original_headline: Apple announces Siri AI and its next generation of Apple Intelligence

stories.headline: Xiaomi's open model now runs at 1,000 tokens per second
stories.blurb: Xiaomi's MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed is a 1-trillion-parameter model that generates text at 1,000 tokens per second—fast enough to feel like instant conversation. The company used custom inference optimizations and their own hardware to hit speeds that typically require much smaller models.
stories.why_it_matters: Large models can now run at speeds that make real-time voice and interactive apps practical.
stories.original_headline: MiMo-v2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed: 1T model with 1000 tokens per second

stories.headline: Microsoft shut down 70 of its own repos after hackers pushed malware targeting AI coding agents
stories.blurb: Microsoft closed more than 70 GitHub repositories it owned after attackers compromised them to distribute credential-stealing malware. The malware specifically targeted developers using AI coding assistants like Claude and Gemini, attempting to harvest login credentials when the agents interacted with the poisoned repos.
stories.why_it_matters: Supply chain attacks now explicitly target AI agents, not just human developers.
stories.original_headline: Microsoft Hacked to Deliver Malware to Claude and Gemini Users

stories.headline: NotebookLM can now run Python code and cite its sources
stories.blurb: Google upgraded NotebookLM to Gemini 3.5, adding two new abilities: it can execute Python code to analyze data in your notes, and it now shows exactly which sources it used for each answer. The AI note-taking tool launched in 2023 and lets you chat with your uploaded documents.
stories.why_it_matters: NotebookLM went from a chat interface to an actual workspace that can crunch numbers and show its work.
stories.original_headline: NotebookLM’s Gemini 3.5 upgrade adds a cloud computer and help finding sources

stories.headline: Amazon now lets you AI-generate a design and print it on a shirt
stories.blurb: The Amazon Shopping app added a feature that lets you tell Alexa what you want on a T-shirt, hoodie, or tumbler, and it generates the design. You can then order the custom merch directly in the app.
stories.why_it_matters: AI design tools are now built into where people already shop for everyday stuff.
stories.original_headline: Amazon now lets you design custom merch using AI

try_this_prompt.title: The negotiation floor
try_this_prompt.prompt: I'm about to negotiate something I'll describe below. Don't tell me tactics. Instead: tell me the worst outcome I should still accept and walk away satisfied, then tell me the one thing I'm probably undervaluing that the other side would give up easily.
try_this_prompt.tool_hint: Before salary talks, vendor contracts, or any deal where you're guessing what's fair.

daily_question: If you could speed-run one AI capability from 'demo' to 'production-ready' this year, which would it be?

args.reason: Apple's Siri AI overhaul at WWDC is a major platform story—two years after the initial Apple Intelligence promise, they're shipping a next-gen Siri. This is concrete, affects millions of users, and passes the screenshot test: "Apple just announced the new Siri AI." Top-tier source, massive consumer impact.

The speed benchmark is specific and impressive—this is the kind of capability jump that makes AI feel like it's accelerating. Different vendor from Apple, appeals to builder audience.
